info.title: CodeIgniter 4 API Starter
info.description: RESTful API built with CodeIgniter 4, featuring JWT authentication, standardized responses, and comprehensive documentation.

post.description: Creates a new API key. The full raw key is returned only once in this response and cannot be retrieved again.

201.description: API key created — raw key included once

get.summary: Get audit logs for an entity

post.description: If the user already exists, Google login succeeds immediately. If the user is new, the account is created in pending_approval and requires admin approval.

202.description: Google login received, account pending admin approval

get.summary: Get the authenticated user (with effective permissions)
get.description: Returns the canonical "me" shape: profile fields plus the effective permission codes used for UI gating. Permissions are resolved fresh from the IAM tables on each call.

patch.summary: Update the authenticated user's own profile
patch.description: Allowlist: first_name, last_name, avatar_url. Email, password and role assignments are not modifiable through self-update. Returns the canonical "me" shape with refreshed permissions.

post.summary: Introspect a JWT issued by this server
post.description: Validates a JWT (signature, expiration, revocation) and returns its claims. Authenticated by X-App-Key — domain apps can verify user tokens without sharing the JWT secret. Always responds 200 with `valid: true|false` unless the calling app itself is unauthorized.
Always returns a generic success response for valid emails. For soft-deleted accounts, this may trigger a reactivation request that requires admin approval.

200.description: Generic success response (reset queued or reactivation requested)

post.description: OAuth client_credentials-style endpoint. The calling domain application authenticates with `X-App-Key` and receives a short-lived JWT whose `sub` is `service:<app_code>` and whose `scope` is the application's permission set. No request body is required.

403.description: X-App-Key invalid, inactive, or not bound to an application

404.description: The application bound to the API key has been deleted

post.description: Issues a fresh access/refresh token pair and returns the canonical authenticated user (including effective permissions) so consumers can re-hydrate UI gating without an extra `/auth/me` call.

post.summary: Revoke all tokens for current user

get.description: By default only non-trashed files are returned. Use `trashed=only` to list the trash bin, or `trashed=with` to include both.

200.description: File metadata (or file download for local storage)

delete.description: Sets `deleted_at` so the file disappears from default listings. Storage bytes are preserved so the file can be restored. To purge permanently, call `DELETE /files/{id}/force` after.

patch.description: Update editable metadata fields. At least one field must be provided. Does not affect the stored binary or variants.

post.description: Uploads a new file to replace the existing one. Preserves the record ID and all references. Deletes the old storage object.

400.description: File is not in the trash

delete.description: Removes storage bytes AND the DB row. Only valid for files already in the trash — call `DELETE /files/{id}` first.

400.description: File is not in the trash

get.summary: List a user's effective permissions for a specific application (by code)

get.description: Lists users except accounts with role superadmin. Requires admin role.

post.description: Admins can create only role=user. Creating admin/superadmin requires superadmin. The account is created as active and an invitation email is sent to set the password.

get.description: Get details of a specific user. Regular users can only access their own profile.

403.description: Forbidden — cannot access another users profile

put.description: Admins can only update users with role=user and cannot promote to admin/superadmin.

delete.description: Admins can delete only users with role=user.

AuthToken.title: Authentication Token
AuthToken.description: JWT token with associated user data

MetricsRequestStats.title: Metrics Request Stats
MetricsRequestStats.description: Aggregated request statistics for a time period

IntrospectRequest.title: Introspect Request
IntrospectRequest.description: JWT to validate via the introspection endpoint

UpdateMeRequest.title: Update Me Request
UpdateMeRequest.description: Fields the authenticated user may modify on their own profile

GalleryAttachRequest.description:
Body for `POST /<resource>/<id>/images`. Attaches an existing file (already
uploaded via the files endpoint) to a parent resource's gallery.

GalleryReorderRequest.description:
Body for `PUT /<resource>/<id>/images/reorder`.

Format: `{ "items": [{ "id": 12, "sort_order": 0 }, { "id": 13, "sort_order": 1 }, ...] }`.
Each entry is a pivot row id paired with its new sort position. Entries
pointing at a different parent are silently ignored by the service.

permission_ids.description: Permission ids to attach to the role

role_ids.description: Role ids to attach to the membership

application_id.description: Application id; null for global roles

permission_ids.description: Optional list of permission ids to attach to the new role. Omit to create a role with no permissions.

application_id.description: Application id; null for global roles

permission_ids.description: Replace the role permission set with this list of permission ids. Omit to leave permissions unchanged. Empty list removes all permissions.

UserCreateRequest.title: User Create Request
UserCreateRequest.description: Data needed to create a new user

role_ids.description: Global role ids to assign to the new user (multi-rol). If empty, the default "user" role is assigned.

UserIndexRequest.title: User Index Request
UserIndexRequest.description: Parameters for filtering and paginating the user list

search.description: Search term for email, first name or last name

UserUpdateRequest.title: User Update Request
UserUpdateRequest.description: Data needed to update an existing user

role_ids.description: Replace the user role set with this list of global role ids. Omit to leave roles unchanged.

name.description: Human-readable label for the API key

key_prefix.description: First characters of the key (safe to display)

is_active.description: Whether the key is currently active

key.description: Full raw API key (only returned once)

user_id.description: ID of user who performed the action

user_email.description: Email of user who performed the action

user_full_name.description: Full name of user who performed the action

valid.description: True if the token is valid, not expired and not revoked

uid.description: User ID from the token (null when invalid)

permissions.description: Effective permission codes carried by the token

LoginResponse.title: Login Response
LoginResponse.description: Successful authentication response containing JWT tokens and user data

MeResponse.title: Authenticated User Response
MeResponse.description: Canonical shape for the authenticated subject. Extends UserResponse with the effective permission codes used for UI gating.

roles.description: Global roles assigned to this user.

permissions.description: Effective permission codes for the current application. Drives UI gating on the frontend.

PendingRegistrationResponse.title: Pending Registration Response
PendingRegistrationResponse.description: Minimal user envelope for accounts awaiting admin approval (e.g. first-time Google sign-in).

ServiceTokenResponse.title: Service Token Response
ServiceTokenResponse.description: JWT issued to a domain application via X-App-Key

access_token.description: Signed JWT (HS256) carrying the application scope

scope.description: Permission codes embedded in the token

GalleryImageResponse.description:
Response payload for a single gallery item: the pivot record enriched with
the underlying file's metadata (original name, image flag, variants).

FileDownloadResponse.title: File Download Response
FileDownloadResponse.description: File metadata for downloads and external storage

category.description: File category derived from MIME type

is_image.description: Whether the file is an image

application_name.description: Display name of the related application

application_id.description: Application id; null for global roles

application_name.description: Display name of the related application

application.description: Summary of the application the permissions are scoped to

permissions.description: Sorted, deduplicated effective permission codes for the user within the application

GoogleIdentityResponse.title: Google Identity Response
GoogleIdentityResponse.description: Verified identity data returned by Google

TokenResponse.title: Token Response
TokenResponse.description:
Token Response DTO

Returned by `POST /auth/refresh`. Carries a fresh access/refresh
token pair plus the canonical authenticated user (`MeResponse`) so
consumers can re-hydrate UI gating without an extra `/auth/me` call.

MetricsOverview.title: Metrics Overview
MetricsOverview.description: Overview of request stats, slow requests, and SLO indicators

payload.description: Raw metrics payload (shape varies by endpoint)

UserResponse.title: User Response
UserResponse.description: User data returned by the API

roles.description: Global roles assigned to this user.

UnauthorizedResponse.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication token

ValidationErrorResponse.description: Validation Error - Request data failed validation

GoogleLoginRequest.description: Google login request with ID token

id_token.description: Google ID token from frontend OAuth flow

password.description: Password - Minimum 8 characters, must contain uppercase, lowercase, and number

CreateUserRequest.description: Data for creating a new user

oauth_provider.description: Optional OAuth provider for externally managed accounts

bearerAuth.description: Enter your JWT token in the format: Bearer {token}

appKeyAuth.description: Per-application API key issued by this server, used to authenticate the calling app (no user JWT required).
appKeyAuth.name: X-App-Key
